title: XOMA Royalty Corporation Declares Quarterly Preferred Stock Dividend and Announces Redemption of Its Perpetual Preferred Stock and CVR Dividend Record Date
author: Globe Newswire
description: XOMA Royalty Corporation announced quarterly preferred stock dividends for Series A and Series B preferred shareholders, with Series A receiving $0.53906 per share and Series B receiving $0.52344 per depositary share, payable on July 15, 2026. The company also announced the redemption of all outstanding preferred stock at $25.00 per share on July 14, 2026, in connection with its pending merger with Ligand Pharmaceuticals through Flex Merger Sub, Inc. Additionally, the record date for CVR distribution to common stockholders has been set for July 13, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement is procedural in nature, declaring routine quarterly dividends and executing a planned redemption as part of an already-announced merger agreement. While the dividend payments and redemption are positive for preferred shareholders, the overall tone is neutral as these are expected corporate actions tied to the pending acquisition.

title: Ligand Announces 2026 Investor Day in New York City
author: Na
description: Ligand Pharmaceuticals announced it will host an Investor Day in New York City on December 8, 2026, featuring presentations from senior management on the company's strategy, investment activity, royalty portfolio, and long-term financial outlook. A live webcast will be available through the company's Investor Relations website.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is proactively hosting an investor day to present its strategy and financial outlook, demonstrating confidence in its business model and portfolio. The announcement highlights the company's diversified royalty portfolio of over 100 assets and positions it as a leading royalty aggregator, which suggests operational strength and growth potential.

title: A Ligand Pharmaceuticals (LGND) Insider Sold 5,000 Shares for $1.1 Million
author: Cory Renauer
description: Andrew Reardon, CLO & Secretary at Ligand Pharmaceuticals, sold 5,000 shares worth approximately $1.14 million on June 1, 2026, through the exercise and immediate disposition of stock options. Post-transaction, Reardon retains 41,382 shares and 39,444 employee stock options. The analyst views this as income supplementation rather than a loss of confidence, noting Ligand's strong 123.61% one-year return and growing royalty revenue (up 56% YoY in Q1), though Captisol sales declined.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company demonstrates strong fundamentals with 123.61% one-year stock price appreciation, surging royalty revenue (56% YoY growth to $43M in Q1), and recent FDA approval of Filspari for FSGS treatment. The insider sale is characterized as income supplementation rather than a loss of confidence, with the executive retaining substantial equity stakes (41,382 shares plus 39,444 options), indicating alignment with shareholder interests.

title: Villere St Denis Liquidates $18 Million Euronet Worldwide Stake, According to Recent SEC Filing
author: Eric Trie
description: Investment fund Villere St Denis J & Co LLC completely exited its position in Euronet Worldwide, selling 244,878 shares worth approximately $17.53 million in Q1 2026. The liquidation represents a full exit from a position that previously comprised 1.9% of the fund's assets under management. Euronet shares have declined 25.4% over the past year, underperforming the S&P 500 significantly.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Major institutional investor completely liquidated its position; stock down 25.4% year-over-year and underperforming S&P 500 by 53.69 percentage points; article highlights structural complexity and profitability concerns across its diversified business lines (ATM/EFT, prepaid distribution, money transfer)

title: XOMA Investors Have the Opportunity to Join Investigation of XOMA Royalty Corporation with the Schall Law Firm
author: Schall Law Firm
description: The Schall Law Firm is investigating potential breaches of fiduciary duty by XOMA Royalty Corporation's board and management regarding the company's $739 million acquisition by Ligand Pharmaceuticals. Shareholders are invited to participate in the investigation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is under investigation for potential breaches of fiduciary duty by its board and management, suggesting governance concerns and potential shareholder harm in the acquisition deal.
